v.CNV: 他用皂荚木做燔祭坛，长两公尺两公寸，宽两公尺两公寸，成正方形，高一公尺三公寸。
v.ESV: He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood. Five cubits was its length, and five cubits its breadth. It was square, and three cubits was its height.
v.GAE: 그가 또 조각목으로 번제단을 만들었으니 길이는 다섯 규빗이요 너비도 다섯 규빗이라 네모가 반듯하고 높이는 세 규빗이며
v.KJV: And he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood: five cubits was the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; it was foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof.
v.NASB: Then he made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood, five cubits long, and five cubits wide, square, and three cubits high.
v.NIV: They built the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood, three cubits high; it was square, five cubits long and five cubits wide.
v.NRSV: He made the altar of burnt offering also of acacia wood; it was five cubits long, and five cubits wide; it was square, and three cubits high.
v.SAENEW: 그는 아카시아 나무로 번제단을 만들었는데, 그 길이가 다섯 자요 너비가 다섯 자인 네모난 모양으로 만들었으며, 그 높이는 석 자로 하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 싯딤 나무로 번제단을 만들었습니다. 그 길이는 5규빗, 너비도 5규빗이 되는 정사각형으로 높이는 3규빗이었습니다.

v.CNV: 在坛的四个拐角做了四只角，角是与坛连在一起的；又用铜把坛包裹。
v.ESV: He made horns for it on its four corners. Its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
v.GAE: 그 네 모퉁이 위에 그 뿔을 만들되 그 뿔을 제단과 연결하게 하고 제단을 놋으로 쌌으며
v.KJV: And he made the horns thereof on the four corners of it; the horns thereof were of the same: and he overlaid it with brass.
v.NASB: He made its horns on its four corners, its horns being of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
v.NIV: They made a horn at each of the four corners, so that the horns and the altar were of one piece, and they overlaid the altar with bronze.
v.NRSV: He made horns for it on its four corners; its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
v.SAENEW: 번제단의 네 모퉁이에 뿔을 하나씩 만들어 붙이고, 그 뿔과 제단을 하나로 잇게 하고, 거기에 놋쇠를 입혔다.
v.WLB: 네 모서리에 각각 뿔을 만들어 뿔과 제단이 한 덩어리가 되게 하고 제단은 청동으로 씌웠습니다.

v.CNV: 又做坛上的一切器具，就是盆、铲、盘、肉叉和火盆；坛上的一切器具都是用铜做的。
v.ESV: And he made all the utensils of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the fire pans. He made all its utensils of bronze.
v.GAE: 제단의 모든 기구 곧 통과 부삽과 대야와 고기 갈고리와 불 옮기는 그릇을 다 놋으로 만들고
v.KJV: And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass.
v.NASB: He made all the utensils of the altar, the pails and the shovels and the basins, the flesh hooks and the firepans; he made all its utensils of bronze.
v.NIV: They made all its utensils of bronze—its pots, shovels, sprinkling bowls, meat forks and firepans.
v.NRSV: He made all the utensils of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the firepans: all its utensils he made of bronze.
v.SAENEW: 번제단의 모든 기구, 곧 재를 담는 통과 부삽과 대야와 고기 갈고리와 불 옮기는 그릇을 놋으로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 재 비우는 통, 부삽, 피 뿌리는 대접, 고기 포크와 불판 등 제단에 쓰이는 모든 기구들도 청동으로 만들었습니다.

v.CNV: 又替坛做一个铜网，放在坛的围腰板以下，向下直到坛的半腰。
v.ESV: And he made for the altar a grating, a network of bronze, under its ledge, extending halfway down.
v.GAE: 제단을 위하여 놋 그물을 만들어 제단 주위 가장자리 아래에 두되 제단 절반에 오르게 하고
v.KJV: And he made for the altar a brasen grate of network under the compass thereof beneath unto the midst of it.
v.NASB: He made for the altar a grating of bronze network beneath, under its ledge, reaching halfway up.
v.NIV: They made a grating for the altar, a bronze network, to be under its ledge, halfway up the altar.
v.NRSV: He made for the altar a grating, a network of bronze, under its ledge, extending halfway down.
v.SAENEW: 제단에 쓸 그물을 놋쇠로 만들고, 제단 가장자리 밑에 달아서, 제단의 중간에까지 이르게 하였다.
v.WLB: 또 제단을 위해 청동으로 된 그물판을 만들어 제단 높이의 2분의 1쯤 올라오도록 제단 밑에 달았습니다.

v.CNV: 又铸造四个环，放在铜网的四端之上，用作抬坛的地方。
v.ESV: He cast four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating as holders for the poles.
v.GAE: 그 놋 그물 네 모퉁이에 채를 꿸 고리 넷을 부어 만들었으며
v.KJV: And he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, to be places for the staves.
v.NASB: He cast four rings on the four ends of the bronze grating as holders for the poles.
v.NIV: They cast bronze rings to hold the poles for the four corners of the bronze grating.
v.NRSV: He cast four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating to hold the poles;
v.SAENEW: 놋그물의 네 모퉁이에, 채를 끼우는 데 쓸 고리 네 개를 부어 만들었다.
v.WLB: 그리고 청동 그물판의 네 모퉁이에 장대를 끼울 청동 고리를 부어 만들고

v.CNV: 他用皂荚木做两根杠，用铜包裹，
v.ESV: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
v.GAE: 채를 조각목으로 만들어 놋으로 싸고
v.KJV: And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with brass.
v.NASB: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
v.NIV: They made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
v.NRSV: he made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with bronze.
v.SAENEW: 아카시아 나무로 채를 만들고, 거기에 놋쇠를 입혔다.
v.WLB: 싯딤 나무로 장대도 만들어 청동으로 씌웠습니다.

v.CNV: 把杠穿在坛两侧的环里，用来抬坛；坛是用木板做的，是空的。
v.ESV: And he put the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar to carry it with them. He made it hollow, with boards.
v.GAE: 제단 양쪽 고리에 그 채를 꿰어 메게 하였으며 제단은 널판으로 속이 비게 만들었더라
v.KJV: And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar, to bear it withal; he made the altar hollow with boards.
v.NASB: He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar, with which to carry it. He made it hollow with planks.
v.NIV: They inserted the poles into the rings so they would be on the sides of the altar for carrying it. They made it hollow, out of boards.
v.NRSV: And he put the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar, to carry it with them; he made it hollow, with boards.
v.SAENEW: 이 채들을 번제단 양 옆의 고리에 끼워서, 그것을 운반할 수 있게 하고, 번제단은 널빤지로 속이 비게 만들었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 제단 양쪽에 장대를 고리 네 개에 끼워 운반할 수 있도록 했고 제단은 널판으로 속이 비게 만들었습니다.

v.CNV: 他用铜做洗濯盆，又用铜做盆座，是用那些在会幕门口服事的妇女的铜镜做成的。
v.ESV: He made the basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the ministering women who ministered in the entrance of the tent of meeting.
v.GAE: 그가 놋으로 물두멍을 만들고 그 받침도 놋으로 하였으니 곧 회막 문에서 수종드는 여인들의 거울로 만들었더라
v.KJV: And he made the laver of brass, and the foot of it of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
v.NASB: Moreover, he made the laver of bronze with its base of bronze, from the mirrors of the serving women who served at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
v.NIV: They made the bronze basin and its bronze stand from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
v.NRSV: He made the basin of bronze with its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
v.SAENEW: 그는 물두멍과 그 받침을 놋쇠로 만들었는데, 그것은 회막 어귀에서 봉사하는 여인들이 바친 놋거울로 만든 것이다.
v.WLB: 그들은 청동으로 대야와 그 받침대를 만들었습니다. 이것은 회막문에 모인 여자들의 거울을 녹여 만든 것입니다.

v.CNV: 他又做院子；南面，就是向南的方向，用捻的细麻做院子的幔幕，共四十四公尺。
v.ESV: And he made the court. For the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits;
v.GAE: 그가 또 뜰을 만들었으니 남으로 뜰의 남쪽에는 세마포 포장이 백 규빗이라
v.KJV: And he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, an hundred cubits:
v.NASB: Then he made the court: for the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits;
v.NIV: Next they made the courtyard. The south side was a hundred cubits long and had curtains of finely twisted linen,
v.NRSV: He made the court; for the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits long;
v.SAENEW: 그는 성막 뜰을 두르는 울타리를 만들었는데, 가는 실로 짠 모시 휘장으로 울타리를 둘렀다. 그 남쪽 휘장은 백 자로 하였다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 그들은 뜰을 만들었습니다. 남쪽은 길이가 100규빗으로 고운 베실로 만든 휘장을 드리웠습니다.

v.CNV: 幔幕的柱子有二十根，柱座有二十个，是铜做的；柱钩和横柱闩是银做的。
v.ESV: their twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
v.GAE: 그 기둥이 스물이며 그 받침이 스물이니 놋이요 기둥의 갈고리와 가름대는 은이며
v.KJV: Their pillars were twenty, and their brasen sockets twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
v.NASB: their twenty pillars, and their twenty sockets, made of bronze; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.NIV: with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases, and with silver hooks and bands on the posts.
v.NRSV: its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.SAENEW: 휘장을 칠 기둥 스무 개와 그 밑받침 스무 개를 놋쇠로 만들고, 그 기둥의 갈고리와 고리를 은으로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 또 기둥이 20개, 청동받침대가 20개 필요했고 기둥의 고리와 가름대는 은으로 만들었습니다.

v.CNV: 北面，有幔幕四十四公尺，幔幕的柱子有二十根，柱座有二十个，是铜做的；柱钩和横柱闩是银做的。
v.ESV: And for the north side there were hangings of a hundred cubits; their twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
v.GAE: 그 북쪽에도 백 규빗이라 그 기둥이 스물이며 그 받침이 스물이니 놋이요 기둥의 갈고리와 가름대는 은이며
v.KJV: And for the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits, their pillars were twenty, and their sockets of brass twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
v.NASB: For the north side there were one hundred cubits; their twenty pillars and their twenty sockets were of bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.NIV: The north side was also a hundred cubits long and had twenty posts and twenty bronze bases, with silver hooks and bands on the posts.
v.NRSV: For the north side there were hangings one hundred cubits long; its twenty pillars and their twenty bases were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.SAENEW: 북쪽에도 마찬가지로, 백 자가 되는 휘장을 치고, 기둥 스무 개와 밑받침 스무 개를 놋쇠로 만들고, 기둥의 갈고리와 고리를 은으로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 북쪽도 역시 길이가 100규빗으로 기둥이 20개, 청동받침대가 20개 필요했고 기둥의 고리와 가름대는 은으로 만들었습니다.

v.CNV: 西面，有幔幕二十二公尺，幔幕的柱子有十根，柱座有十个；柱钩和横柱闩是银做的。
v.ESV: And for the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their ten pillars, and their ten bases; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
v.GAE: 서쪽에 포장은 쉰 규빗이라 그 기둥이 열이요 받침이 열이며 기둥의 갈고리와 가름대는 은이며
v.KJV: And for the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
v.NASB: For the west side there were hangings of fifty cubits with their ten pillars and their ten sockets; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.NIV: The west end was fifty cubits wide and had curtains, with ten posts and ten bases, with silver hooks and bands on the posts.
v.NRSV: For the west side there were hangings fifty cubits long, with ten pillars and ten bases; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
v.SAENEW: 해 지는 쪽인 서쪽 울타리는 쉰 자가 되는 휘장으로 하고, 기둥 열 개와 밑받침 열 개를 만들었다. 그 기둥의 갈고리와 고리를 은으로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 서쪽은 폭이 50규빗으로 휘장이 걸렸고 기둥이 열 개, 받침대가 열 개, 기둥의 고리와 가름대는 은으로 만들었습니다.

v.CNV: 东面，就是日出的方向，有幔幕二十二公尺。
v.ESV: And for the front to the east, fifty cubits.
v.GAE: 동으로 동쪽에도 쉰 규빗이라
v.KJV: And for the east side eastward fifty cubits.
v.NASB: For the east side fifty cubits.
v.NIV: The east end, toward the sunrise, was also fifty cubits wide.
v.NRSV: And for the front to the east, fifty cubits.
v.SAENEW: 해 뜨는 쪽인 동쪽 울타리도 쉰 자로 하였다.
v.WLB: 동쪽의 폭 역시 50규빗이었습니다.

v.CNV: 门口这一边有幔幕六公尺六公寸；幔幕的柱子有三根，柱座有三个。
v.ESV: The hangings for one side of the gate were fifteen cubits, with their three pillars and three bases.
v.GAE: 문 이쪽의 포장이 열다섯 규빗이요 그 기둥이 셋이요 받침이 셋이며
v.KJV: The hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
v.NASB: The hangings for the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits, with their three pillars and their three sockets,
v.NIV: Curtains fifteen cubits long were on one side of the entrance, with three posts and three bases,
v.NRSV: The hangings for one side of the gate were fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
v.SAENEW: 동쪽의 정문 한쪽에 밑받침 셋을 놓고, 그 위에 기둥 셋을 세운 다음에, 열다섯 자가 되는 휘장을 쳤다.
v.WLB: 뜰 오른쪽에는 길이 15규빗짜리 휘장이 걸렸고 기둥 세 개와 받침대 세 개가 세워졌습니다.

v.CNV: 门口那一边也是这样。院子门口的两边各有幔幕六公尺六公寸；幔幕的柱子有三根，柱座有三个。
v.ESV: And so for the other side. On both sides of the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits, with their three pillars and their three bases.
v.GAE: 문 저쪽도 그와 같으니 뜰 문 이쪽, 저쪽의 포장이 열다섯 규빗씩이요 그 기둥이 셋씩, 받침이 셋씩이라
v.KJV: And for the other side of the court gate, on this hand and that hand, were hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
v.NASB: and so for the other side. On both sides of the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits, with their three pillars and their three sockets.
v.NIV: and curtains fifteen cubits long were on the other side of the entrance to the courtyard, with three posts and three bases.
v.NRSV: And so for the other side; on each side of the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.
v.SAENEW: 다른 한쪽에도 밑받침 셋을 놓고서, 그 위에 기둥 셋을 세운 다음에, 열다섯 자가 되는 휘장을 쳤다. 동쪽 울타리에 있는 정문의 양쪽을 이렇게 똑같이 만들었다.
v.WLB: 뜰 문 왼쪽에도 길이 15규빗짜리 휘장이 걸렸고 그와 함께 기둥 세 개와 받침대 세 개가 세워졌습니다.

v.CNV: 院子周围的一切幔幕都是用捻的细麻做的。
v.ESV: All the hangings around the court were of fine twined linen.
v.GAE: 뜰 주위의 포장은 세마포요
v.KJV: All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen.
v.NASB: All the hangings of the court all around were of fine twisted linen.
v.NIV: All the curtains around the courtyard were of finely twisted linen.
v.NRSV: All the hangings around the court were of fine twisted linen.
v.SAENEW: 울타리의 사면을 두른 휘장은 모두 가늘게 꼰 모시 실로 짠 것이다.
v.WLB: 뜰을 둘러싼 모든 휘장은 고운 베실로 만들었고

v.CNV: 柱座是铜做的，柱钩和横柱闩是银做的，柱顶是用银包裹的；院子的一切柱子都是用银横闩相连起来。
v.ESV: And the bases for the pillars were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver. The overlaying of their capitals was also of silver, and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.
v.GAE: 기둥 받침은 놋이요 기둥의 갈고리와 가름대는 은이요 기둥 머리 싸개는 은이며 뜰의 모든 기둥에 은 가름대를 꿰었으며
v.KJV: And the sockets for the pillars were of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.
v.NASB: The sockets for the pillars were of bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their bands, of silver; and the overlaying of their tops, of silver, and all the pillars of the court were furnished with silver bands.
v.NIV: The bases for the posts were bronze. The hooks and bands on the posts were silver, and their tops were overlaid with silver; so all the posts of the courtyard had silver bands.
v.NRSV: The bases for the pillars were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver; the overlaying of their capitals was also of silver, and all the pillars of the court were banded with silver.
v.SAENEW: 기둥 밑받침은 놋쇠로 만들었으나, 기둥 갈고리와 고리는 은으로 만들고, 기둥머리 덮개는 은으로 씌웠다. 울타리의 모든 기둥에는 은고리를 달았다.
v.WLB: 기둥 받침대는 청동으로 만들었으며 기둥의 고리와 가름대는 은이었고 기둥의 머리는 은을 씌운 것이었습니다. 뜰의 모든 기둥에는 은가름대가 꽂혔습니다.

v.CNV: 院子的门帘，是用蓝色紫色朱红色线和捻的细麻，以及刺绣的手工做成的；长九公尺，以宽度来说，两公尺高，与院子的幔幕相配。
v.ESV: And the screen for the gate of the court was embroidered with needlework in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen. It was twenty cubits long and five cubits high in its breadth, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
v.GAE: 뜰의 휘장 문을 청색 자색 홍색 실과 가늘게 꼰 베 실로 수 놓아 짰으니 길이는 스무 규빗이요 너비와 높이는 뜰의 포장과 같이 다섯 규빗이며
v.KJV: And the hanging for the gate of the court was needlework, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: and twenty cubits was the length, and the height in the breadth was five cubits, answerable to the hangings of the court.
v.NASB: The screen of the gate of the court was the work of the weaver, of blue and purple and scarlet material and fine twisted linen. And the length was twenty cubits and the height was five cubits, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
v.NIV: The curtain for the entrance to the courtyard was made of blue, purple and scarlet yarn and finely twisted linen—the work of an embroiderer. It was twenty cubits long and, like the curtains of the courtyard, five cubits high,
v.NRSV: The screen for the entrance to the court was embroidered with needlework in blue, purple, and crimson yarns and fine twisted linen. It was twenty cubits long and, along the width of it, five cubits high, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
v.SAENEW: 동쪽 울타리의 정문에 칠 막은, 청색 실과 자주색 실과 홍색 실과 가늘게 꼰 모시 실로 수를 놓아 짠 것으로, 그 길이는 스무 자이고, 너비 곧 높이는 뜰의 휘장과 마찬가지로 다섯 자이다.
v.WLB: 뜰 문이 되는 휘장은 청색 실, 자주색 실, 홍색 실과 고운 베실을 수놓아 짠 것으로 길이가 20규빗이고 높이는 뜰 주위에 둘러 걸린 휘장과 마찬가지로 5규빗이었으며

v.CNV: 幔幕的柱子有四根，柱座有四个，是铜做的；柱钩是银做的，柱顶和横柱闩是用银包裹的。
v.ESV: And their pillars were four in number. Their four bases were of bronze, their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their fillets of silver.
v.GAE: 그 기둥은 넷인데 그 받침 넷은 놋이요 그 갈고리는 은이요 그 머리 싸개와 가름대도 은이며
v.KJV: And their pillars were four, and their sockets of brass four; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their chapiters and their fillets of silver.
v.NASB: Their four pillars and their four sockets were of bronze; their hooks were of silver, and the overlaying of their tops and their bands were of silver.
v.NIV: with four posts and four bronze bases. Their hooks and bands were silver, and their tops were overlaid with silver.
v.NRSV: There were four pillars; their four bases were of bronze, their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their bands of silver.
v.SAENEW: 그것을 칠 기둥 네 개와 그 밑받침 네 개를 놋쇠로 만들고, 그 갈고리를 은으로 만들고, 기둥머리 덮개와 고리를 은으로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 거기에는 기둥 네 개와 청동받침대 네 개가 세워졌습니다. 기둥의 고리와 가름대는 은이며 기둥 머리는 은으로 씌운 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 帐幕的一切钉子和院子四周的一切钉子，都是铜做的。
v.ESV: And all the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court all around were of bronze.
v.GAE: 성막 말뚝과 뜰 주위의 말뚝은 모두 놋이더라
v.KJV: And all the pins of the tabernacle, and of the court round about, were of brass.
v.NASB: All the pegs of the tabernacle and of the court all around were of bronze.
v.NIV: All the tent pegs of the tabernacle and of the surrounding courtyard were bronze.
v.NRSV: All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court all around were of bronze.
v.SAENEW: 성막의 말뚝과 울타리 사면에 박을 말뚝은 모두 놋쇠로 만들었다.
v.WLB: 성막과 주변 뜰의 말뚝은 모두 청동이었습니다.

v.CNV: 这是帐幕（就是法柜的帐幕）内利未人使用的物件的总数，是照着摩西的吩咐，经亚伦祭司的儿子以他玛的手计算的。
v.ESV: These are the records of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, as they were recorded at the commandment of Moses, the responsibility of the Levites under the direction of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
v.GAE: 성막 곧 증거막을 위하여 레위 사람이 쓴 재료의 물목은 제사장 아론의 아들 이다말이 모세의 명령대로 계산하였으며
v.KJV: This is the sum of the tabernacle, even of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son to Aaron the priest.
v.NASB: This is the number of the things for the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, as they were numbered according to the command of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
v.NIV: These are the amounts of the materials used for the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the covenant law, which were recorded at Moses’ command by the Levites under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron, the priest.
v.NRSV: These are the records of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the covenant, which were drawn up at the commandment of Moses, the work of the Levites being under the direction of Ithamar son of the priest Aaron.
v.SAENEW: 다음은 성막 곧 증거판을 간직한 성막 공사의 명세서로서, 제사장 아론의 아들 이다말이 모세의 명령을 받아, 레위 사람들을 시켜서 계산한 것이다.
v.WLB: 다음은 성막, 곧 증거막에 사용한 재료들의 양입니다. 이것은 모세의 명령에 따라 제사장 아론의 아들 이다말의 지도하에 레위 사람들이 기록한 것입니다.

v.CNV: 凡是耶和华吩咐摩西的，犹大支派户珥的孙子、乌利的儿子比撒列都做了。
v.ESV: Bezalel the son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the Lord commanded Moses;
v.GAE: 유다 지파 훌의 손자요 우리의 아들인 브살렐은 여호와께서 모세에게 명령하신 모든 것을 만들었고
v.KJV: And Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses.
v.NASB: Now Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD had commanded Moses.
v.NIV: (Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made everything the Lord commanded Moses;
v.NRSV: Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses;
v.SAENEW: 유다 지파 사람 훌의 손자이며 우리의 아들인 브살렐은, 주님께서 모세에게 명하신 모든 것을 만들었다.
v.WLB: 유다 지파인 훌의 손자이며 우리의 아들인 브살렐이 여호와께서 모세에게 명령하신 모든 것을 만들었는데

v.CNV: 与他同工的，有但支派亚希撒抹的儿子亚何利亚伯，是个雕刻匠、图案设计家，以及能用蓝色紫色朱红色线和细麻刺绣的人。
v.ESV: and with him was Oholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver and designer and embroiderer in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.
v.GAE: 단 지파 아히사막의 아들 오홀리압이 그와 함께 하였으니 오홀리압은 재능이 있어서 조각하며 또 청색 자색 홍색 실과 가는 베 실로 수 놓은 자더라
v.KJV: And with him was Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen.
v.NASB: With him was Oholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver and a skillful workman and a weaver in blue and in purple and in scarlet material, and fine linen.
v.NIV: with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamak, of the tribe of Dan—an engraver and designer, and an embroiderer in blue, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen.)
v.NRSV: and with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and crimson yarns, and in fine linen.
v.SAENEW: 그를 도와서 함께 일한 단 지파 사람 아히사막의 아들 오홀리압은, 조각도 하고, 도안도 그렸으며, 청색 실과 자주색 실과 홍색 실과 가는 모시 실로 수를 놓는 일도 하였다.
v.WLB: 단 지파 아히사막의 아들 오홀리압이 브살렐과 함께했습니다. 오홀리압은 조각도 하고 고안도 하고 청색 실, 자주색 실, 홍색 실과 고운 베실로 수도 놓는 사람이었습니다.

v.CNV: 用来建造圣所整个工程使用的一切黄金，就是献作礼物的金子，按照圣所的标准，共有一千公斤。
v.ESV: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, the gold from the offering, was twenty-nine talents and 730 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary.
v.GAE: 성소 건축 비용으로 들인 금은 성소의 세겔로 스물아홉 달란트와 칠백삼십 세겔이며
v.KJV: All the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place, even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary.
v.NASB: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the work of the sanctuary, even the gold of the wave offering, was 29 talents and 730 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
v.NIV: The total amount of the gold from the wave offering used for all the work on the sanctuary was 29 talents and 730 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
v.NRSV: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, the gold from the offering, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred thirty shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel.
v.SAENEW: 성소 건축비로 든 금 곧 흔들어 바친 금은 모두 성소 세겔로 이십구 달란트 칠백삼십 세겔이다.
v.WLB: 성소를 짓는 데 요제로 드린 금은 성소 세겔로 해서 모두 합하면 29달란트 730세겔이었습니다.

v.CNV: 会众中被数点的人所出的银子，按照圣所的标准，共有三千四百三十公斤。
v.ESV: The silver from those of the congregation who were recorded was a hundred talents and 1,775 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary:
v.GAE: 계수된 회중이 드린 은은 성소의 세겔로 백 달란트와 천칠백칠십오 세겔이니
v.KJV: And the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:
v.NASB: The silver of those of the congregation who were numbered was 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary;
v.NIV: The silver obtained from those of the community who were counted in the census was 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel—
v.NRSV: The silver from those of the congregation who were counted was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, measured by the sanctuary shekel;
v.SAENEW: 인구 조사의 대상이 된 회중이 바친 은은 성소의 세겔로 백 달란트 천칠백칠십오 세겔이다.
v.WLB: 인구 조사에 등록된 사람들에게 받은 은은 성소 세겔로 해서 100달란트 1,775세겔이었습니다.

v.CNV: 凡是按着次序被数点的人，从二十岁以上的，有六十万三千五百五十人，按着圣所的标准，每人纳银六克。
v.ESV: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, by the shekel of the sanctuary), for everyone who was listed in the records, from twenty years old and upward, for 603,550 men.
v.GAE: 계수된 자가 이십 세 이상으로 육십만 삼천오백오십 명인즉 성소의 세겔로 각 사람에게 은 한 베가 곧 반 세겔씩이라
v.KJV: A bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men.
v.NASB: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary), for each one who passed over to those who were numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for 603,550 men.
v.NIV: one beka per person, that is, half a shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, from everyone who had crossed over to those counted, twenty years old or more, a total of 603,550 men.
v.NRSV: a beka a head (that is, half a shekel, measured by the sanctuary shekel), for everyone who was counted in the census, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand, five hundred fifty men.
v.SAENEW: 스무 살이 넘어서 인구 조사의 대상이 된 사람이 모두 육십만 삼천오백오십 명이므로, 한 사람당 성소 세겔로 반 세겔 곧 한 베가씩 낸 셈이다.
v.WLB: 한 사람당 1베가씩, 곧 성소 세겔로 해서 2분의 1세겔씩, 20세 이상으로 등록된 모든 사람 총 60만 3,550명에게서 거둔 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他用这三千四百公斤银子铸造圣所的柱座，和幔子的柱座；一百个柱座用了三千四百公斤银子，三十四公斤银子做一个柱座。
v.ESV: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil; a hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent a base.
v.GAE: 은 백 달란트로 성소의 받침과 휘장 문의 기둥 받침을 모두 백 개를 부어 만들었으니 각 받침마다 한 달란트씩 모두 백 달란트요
v.KJV: And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
v.NASB: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
v.NIV: The 100 talents of silver were used to cast the bases for the sanctuary and for the curtain—100 bases from the 100 talents, one talent for each base.
v.NRSV: The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary, and the bases of the curtain; one hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent for a base.
v.SAENEW: 성소 밑받침과 휘장 밑받침을 부어 만드는 데 은 백 달란트가 들었으니, 밑받침 백 개에 백 달란트 곧 밑받침 한 개에 한 달란트가 든 셈이다.
v.WLB: 성소 받침대와 휘장 받침대를 부어 만드는 데는 은 100달란트가 들었는데 100달란트로 받침대 100개를 만들었으니 받침대 하나 당 1달란트가 든 셈입니다.

v.CNV: 他用三十公斤银子做柱钩，包裹柱顶，以及为柱顶做横柱闩。
v.ESV: And of the 1,775 shekels he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their capitals and made fillets for them.
v.GAE: 천칠백칠십오 세겔로 기둥 갈고리를 만들고 기둥 머리를 싸고 기둥 가름대를 만들었으며
v.KJV: And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them.
v.NASB: Of the 1,775 shekels, he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their tops and made bands for them.
v.NIV: They used the 1,775 shekels to make the hooks for the posts, to overlay the tops of the posts, and to make their bands.
v.NRSV: Of the thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their capitals and made bands for them.
v.SAENEW: 천칠백칠십오 세겔을 들여서, 기둥의 갈고리와 기둥 머리의 덮개와 기둥의 고리를 만들었다.
v.WLB: 기둥의 고리를 만들고 기둥 머리를 도금하는 것과 그 가름대를 만드는 데 1,775세겔이 들었습니다.

v.CNV: 献作礼物的铜有二千四百二十五公斤。
v.ESV: The bronze that was offered was seventy talents and 2,400 shekels;
v.GAE: 드린 놋은 칠십 달란트와 이천사백 세겔이라
v.KJV: And the brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels.
v.NASB: The bronze of the wave offering was 70 talents and 2,400 shekels.
v.NIV: The bronze from the wave offering was 70 talents and 2,400 shekels.
v.NRSV: The bronze that was contributed was seventy talents, and two thousand four hundred shekels;
v.SAENEW: 흔들어 바친 놋쇠는 칠십 달란트 이천사백 세겔인데,
v.WLB: 요제의 예물로 바쳐진 청동은 70달란트 2,400세겔이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他用这些铜做会幕门口的柱座、铜坛和坛上的铜网，以及坛上的一切器具、
v.ESV: with it he made the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar and the bronze grating for it and all the utensils of the altar,
v.GAE: 이것으로 회막 문 기둥 받침과 놋 제단과 놋 그물과 제단의 모든 기구를 만들었으며
v.KJV: And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar,
v.NASB: With it he made the sockets to the doorway of the tent of meeting, and the bronze altar and its bronze grating, and all the utensils of the altar,
v.NIV: They used it to make the bases for the entrance to the tent of meeting, the bronze altar with its bronze grating and all its utensils,
v.NRSV: with it he made the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar and the bronze grating for it and all the utensils of the altar,
v.SAENEW: 이것으로 회막 어귀의 밑받침과 놋제단과 이에 딸린 놋그물과 기타 제단의 모든 기구를 만들고,
v.WLB: 이것을 사용해 회막문 받침대, 청동제단과 청동그물판과 그 기구들과

v.CNV: 院子四周的柱座、院子门口的柱座、帐幕的一切钉子和院子四周的一切钉子。
v.ESV: the bases around the court, and the bases of the gate of the court, all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs around the court.
v.GAE: 뜰 주위의 기둥 받침과 그 휘장 문의 기둥 받침이며 성막의 모든 말뚝과 뜰 주위의 모든 말뚝을 만들었더라
v.KJV: And the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about.
v.NASB: and the sockets of the court all around and the sockets of the gate of the court, and all the pegs of the tabernacle and all the pegs of the court all around.
v.NIV: the bases for the surrounding courtyard and those for its entrance and all the tent pegs for the tabernacle and those for the surrounding courtyard.
v.NRSV: the bases all around the court, and the bases of the gate of the court, all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs around the court.
v.SAENEW: 울타리 사면의 밑받침과 뜰 정문의 밑받침과 성막의 모든 말뚝과 뜰 사면의 모든 말뚝을 만들었다.
v.WLB: 뜰 기둥의 받침대, 뜰 문 기둥의 받침대, 성막 말뚝, 뜰 주변의 말뚝을 만들었습니다.
